metadata.subject: Physical and Health Education
metadata.description: Physical and Health Education 11 challenges students to take full ownership of their physical activity choices and fitness development. Students independently design and lead fitness programs, analyze the science of training, and engage with complex health topics including mental health systems, substance use disorders, reproductive health, and advocacy for community wellness. A growing emphasis on peer leadership, health mentorship, and evidence-based decision-making prepares students for senior leadership roles. First Peoples perspectives on holistic wellness, land-based activity, and community health care are woven throughout.
metadata.general_description: A senior PHE course developing independent fitness leadership, advanced health literacy, peer health advocacy, and evidence-based wellness decision-making — building the capacity for lifelong healthy living and community health contribution.

big_ideas.statement: Daily participation in different types of physical activity influences our physical literacy and personal health and fitness goals.
big_ideas.elaboration: At Grade 11, students exercise greater autonomy in curating their physical activity lives — selecting, designing, and sustaining activity patterns that align with personal goals, values, and the realities of young adult life.

big_ideas.statement: Lifelong participation in physical activity has many benefits and is an essential part of a healthy lifestyle.
big_ideas.elaboration: Senior students examine research-supported evidence for the long-term benefits of physical activity on physical health, mental well-being, and quality of life — and begin connecting current habits to projected future health.

big_ideas.statement: Healthy choices influence our physical, emotional, and mental well-being.
big_ideas.elaboration: Grade 11 students engage with the full complexity of health decision-making — including the social, systemic, and environmental factors that constrain or enable healthy choices for different people.

big_ideas.statement: Healthy relationships can help us lead rewarding and fulfilling lives.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students examine what sustains healthy long-term relationships, how power and communication dynamics affect relationship health, and how to support peers experiencing relationship difficulties.

big_ideas.statement: Advocating for the health and well-being of others connects us to our community.
big_ideas.elaboration: Grade 11 students develop meaningful peer health advocacy skills — learning to recognize need, respond appropriately, reduce stigma, and contribute to a healthier school and community culture.

competencies.raw: Develop, refine, and apply fundamental movement skills in various physical activities; develop and apply movement concepts and strategies in different activities; apply methods for monitoring and adjusting exertion levels; demonstrate safety, fair play, and leadership in physical activities; identify preferred types of physical activity.
i_can_statements: I can independently apply and refine movement skills and strategies across a wide range of physical activities, adapting to different demands.
I can use tactical and strategic thinking to contribute to team and individual performance.
I can design and use a personal exertion monitoring system — including heart rate zones and training load — to guide my activity.
I can model and promote safety, fair play, and leadership as a senior student in physical activity contexts.
I can articulate my physical activity identity, preferences, and plans for sustaining activity throughout my life.

competencies.raw: Participate daily in fitness-enhancing physical activity; describe how school, home, and community activities influence health; develop strategies for promoting healthy eating choices; assess factors influencing healthy choices; identify and analyze health message sources and their behavioural influence; identify and apply personal healthy-living goal strategies; reflect on outcomes and assess personal goal strategies.
i_can_statements: I can independently design and sustain a personal fitness program that meets evidence-based activity guidelines.
I can analyze how systemic and environmental factors — beyond individual choices — shape health outcomes for different people.
I can critically evaluate nutrition information and develop practical, sustainable healthy eating strategies for young adults.
I can deconstruct health messages in media and marketing, identifying rhetorical strategies, biases, and commercial interests.
I can design, implement, and critically evaluate a long-term personal healthy-living plan, including strategies for overcoming barriers.

competencies.raw: Propose strategies for avoiding and/or responding to unsafe or exploitative situations; propose strategies for responding to discrimination, stereotyping, and bullying; propose strategies for developing and maintaining healthy relationships; create strategies for promoting school and community well-being.
i_can_statements: I can analyze unsafe and exploitative situations with depth and develop effective response strategies for myself and peers.
I can lead efforts to challenge discrimination, stereotyping, and harassment in my school and community.
I can support peers in developing and maintaining healthy, respectful, and consensual relationships.
I can design and implement meaningful health promotion strategies within my school or community.

competencies.raw: Describe and assess strategies for promoting mental well-being; describe and assess strategies for managing mental health and substance use problems; create and assess strategies for managing physical, emotional, and social changes during puberty; explore and describe transition and change impacts on identities.
i_can_statements: I can evaluate a broad range of personal and systemic strategies for promoting mental well-being and reducing mental health stigma.
I can analyze mental health disorders and substance use issues with clinical accuracy and design effective support and intervention strategies.
I can support peers navigating mental health challenges by applying active listening, anti-stigma communication, and referral skills.
I can examine how major life transitions — leaving school, entering work or post-secondary — intersect with identity and mental wellness.

areas.name: Physical Activity and Movement
areas.raw: Advanced movement skill application, physical activity leadership, and performance analysis.
topics: Advanced skill refinement: sport-specific technique analysis and performance feedback
Physical activity leadership: planning, leading, and evaluating physical activity sessions for peers
Activity variety: participation in lifetime activities — golf, badminton, swimming, hiking, yoga, weight training
Outdoor and adventure activities: environmental stewardship and safety in outdoor physical pursuits
First Peoples land-based physical activities: traditional games, walking, and connection to place
Coaching and officiating fundamentals: rules knowledge, communication, and leadership in activity

areas.name: Fitness Leadership and Program Design
areas.raw: Advanced fitness planning, periodization, training science, and fitness leadership.
topics: Fitness assessment: conducting and interpreting standardized fitness tests (e.g., VO2 max estimates, 1RM)
Periodization: understanding training cycles — macrocycle, mesocycle, and microcycle planning
Advanced FITT application: designing progressive overload, taper, and recovery phases
Sport-specific training: designing programs for strength, cardiovascular fitness, flexibility, or sport performance
Injury prevention: screening movement patterns, recognizing overtraining, and safe programming
Fitness program leadership: designing and leading group fitness sessions for peers

areas.name: Health Literacy and Decision Making
areas.raw: Advanced health topics including reproductive health, substance use disorders, and health advocacy.
topics: Reproductive health: contraception options, fertility awareness, STI management, and accessing sexual health services
Pregnancy and parenting: options, support systems, and the social context of adolescent pregnancy
Substance use disorders: biological mechanisms of addiction, risk factors, and treatment models
Alcohol, cannabis, and illicit drugs: pharmacological effects, legal context, and harm reduction
Prescription drug misuse: opioids, stimulants, and the health risks of non-prescribed use
Evaluating health research: understanding study design, statistical claims, and evidence quality
Health systems navigation: understanding BC health services, mental health resources, and how to access help
Digital health and wellness apps: evaluating tools for fitness tracking, mental health support, and health information

areas.name: Social and Community Health
areas.raw: Peer health advocacy, healthy relationships, community wellness, and anti-stigma.
topics: Peer health support: active listening, crisis recognition, and appropriate referral skills
Anti-stigma and mental health advocacy: reducing barriers to help-seeking in school communities
Healthy relationship education: communication, power dynamics, conflict resolution, and long-term sustainability
Sexual and gender diversity: LGBTQ+ inclusion, supporting diverse peers, and understanding gender identity
Community health promotion: designing and implementing school or community wellness initiatives
Health equity: understanding how income, race, gender, and location affect health outcomes
First Peoples community health practices and the impacts of colonization on health

areas.name: Mental Well-being
areas.raw: Mental health disorders, suicide prevention, resilience, identity, and transition.
topics: Mental health disorders: anxiety disorders, depression, eating disorders, and psychotic conditions — symptoms and treatment
Suicide awareness and prevention: warning signs, how to have supportive conversations, and safe messaging
Trauma and post-traumatic stress: understanding trauma responses and trauma-informed support
Mindfulness and evidence-based well-being practices: meditation, cognitive behavioural strategies, and self-compassion
Identity in senior secondary: navigating independence, relationships, and future uncertainty
Sleep and physical recovery: the science of sleep and its role in mental health and physical performance
Transitions and resilience: preparing psychologically for life after high school
